title: Re-verify live confidence gate after restart and identify current bypass path

context.operator_report: After restart, the bot is still opening trades around 30% confidence.
current_runtime_evidence: runtime/state.json now shows open trade 241 confidence=0.3863
runtime/state.json now shows open trade 243 confidence=0.3279
runtime/state.json now shows open trade 244 confidence=0.3769
context.problem_statement: The 0.60 confidence floor is still not protecting live entries after restart, so the gate chain is still broken or bypassed in current runtime.

objective: Determine exactly why sub-0.60 trades are still opening after the latest restart, and identify the current live bypass path or configuration/runtime mismatch.
questions: 1. What exact restart/startup line corresponds to the current runtime state with trades 241/243/244?
2. Did trades 241, 243, and 244 open after that restart?
3. Are BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates] lines appearing at all in the current post-restart window?
4. Is MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE still 0.60 in the effective runtime path?
5. What exact current bypass path or runtime mismatch allowed these sub-0.60 trades to open?
6. Is the duplicate-slug issue still the main culprit, or is there a broader live bypass still present?
acceptance_criteria: Explicit YES/NO on current gate rejection evidence
Explicit root cause conclusion for the current live rebreak
